demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse who is the primary carer

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I am absolutely exhausted but my brain simply won't switch off; I lie awake for hours and then wake up at 3 AM every single day. My husband is at the end of his tether — neither of us has slept properly for nights.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Three nights of barely any sleep, I'm agitated and foggy in the day, and I'm dreading going to bed again tonight — we didn't want to wait until the surgery opens.

symptom_narrative.onset: long-standing trouble sleeping that has become much worse over the last several nights
symptom_narrative.duration: escalating over recent weeks, with three very bad nights in a row
symptom_narrative.character: takes 2-3 hours to fall asleep with the mind racing, wakes repeatedly through the night, and is wide awake at about 3 AM unable to get back to sleep
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: says it is the worst it has been and she feels wrung out and on edge all day
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: not applicable — it is the sleep and the tiredness
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: every night; early-morning waking around 3 AM; sleepy in the day but cannot nap
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing seems to help; lying in bed just makes the worrying worse
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: watching the clock, worrying about not sleeping, and dreading bedtime
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: severe daytime tiredness, poor concentration, forgetfulness and short temper that the spouse says is out of character; a lot of anxiety focused on the sleep itself
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: has always been a light sleeper but never this bad
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: usually manages independently but is currently too exhausted and unfocused to get through the day normally

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: mild arthritis that aches a bit but is not what is keeping her awake

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a new antidepressant tablet started recently, taken in the evening

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with her husband who is exhausted from caring for her through the sleepless nights; the couple are worn down and worried

objective_data_offered.value: husband says she has been very tired, foggy, forgetful and short-tempered in the day, which is not like her, and that neither of them is sleeping
husband says she stays fully aware of who he is and where she is — she is just exhausted and anxious, not confused or seeing things
cluttered bedroom with a clock she watches through the night
